text:
3. Join Trellis reconciliation events to NMP kernel outcomes. NMP already applies Trellis ResourcePlan as ApplyDependentInterestDelta; now it needs to retain/export bounded diagnostic
     records instead of test-only traces. [...] `docs/decisions/0075-trellis-private-reconciliation-substrate.md` in nostr-multi-platform. Decision: NMP may use Trellis as *private* reconciliation machinery below typed read sessions ("Trellis owns generic mechanics. NMP owns Nostr and product meaning."). Draws one boundary: Trellis primitives must never become app/native/web-facing or appear in builder docs; NMP owns resource identity semantics even where Trellis owns identity mechanics. "Boundaries" forbids "exported Trellis types in app/native/web APIs" with no carve-out for a diagnostic/tooling surface; "Enforcement" says doctrine tests/public API checks reject raw Trellis primitives in "app/native/web-facing NMP surfaces" — silent on any dev/diagnostic surface. Does not currently mention `nmp-devtools`, receipts, or diagnostics at all. This is exactly the gap #2809 proposes to close by adding a second, dev-build-only "diagnostic surface" category. Related links: ADR-0070 (typed read sessions), ADR-0076 (app-facing feed helpers), epics #2626/#2627 (both closed). [...] - **Phase B — nmp-devtools stream**: ordered open/replace/refresh/close events with owner/provenance/revision/refcount/teardown data; join Trellis `ResourcePlan` → `ApplyDependentInterestDelta` outcomes into bounded retained records (today they're test-only traces); correlate with the existing relay diagnostics; and define the **NMP-owned receipt shape** so Chirp never sees `ResourcePlan`/`ResourceKey`/`Graph`.
